title: Bolbitis inconstans
taxonomy: Bolbitis inconstans is a species of fern in the family Lomariopsidaceae. It belongs to the genus Bolbitis, which is a group of tropical and subtropical ferns. Within the Lomariopsidaceae family, Bolbitis inconstans is classified as a member of the order Polypodiales. This classification reflects the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other ferns. The specific classification of Bolbitis inconstans is also known as Bolbitis inconstans (Blume) Ching.
uses: Bolbitis inconstans has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's extracts are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and swelling. In traditional medicine, Bolbitis inconstans is also used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. Additionally, its extracts have been studied for their potential antimicrobial properties, which may help combat infections. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Bolbitis inconstans for medicinal uses.

intro: Bolbitis inconstans is a species of fern, commonly known as the 'Hairy Bolbitis', belonging to the family Lomariopsidaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress.
